title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Oliver and Hercule Poirot discuss the existence of both a forged and a real codicil regarding Lady Llewellyn-Smith's estate. Mrs. Oliver suggests the real codicil might be hidden in a book titled "In Search of All Mysteries" at Stone Court, though she notes that most of the estate's contents were sold after Llewellyn-Smith's death. Mrs. Oliver questions if Mrs. Lymon was coerced into lying and expresses interest in the disappearance of Olga. Poirot reveals his assistant in London is gathering information and expects news from Bosnia, which may help him learn if Olga has returned or find letters she wrote to a confidante. Mrs. Oliver also mentions a strangled schoolteacher previously noted by Elizabeth Whitaker, speculating on the possibility of murder.

Later, Pollock (referred to as Poirot in the text) walks through a path in a stone quarry garden that appears to have been widened by someone larger than Miranda Butler. He reflects on the garden's eerie, pagan atmosphere and the certainty that the will sent for examination was a forgery, noting that the lawyer Mr. Fulleton is too capable to suggest legal action without evidence. While walking, he encounters Michael Garfield sketching in the garden and Miranda Butler standing by a nearby stream. Poirot observes Michael's skill at pencil sketching and engages him in conversation. Michael explains that he draws to record things before they disappear, specifically mentioning Miranda as a subject he wishes to remember. Miranda approaches, claiming her presence by the stream is a coincidence and not a planned modeling session.
